WRITEUP
CAPESIZE
The market opened the week on a generally subdued note, with overall sentiment leaning softer outside of the Pacific basin. The BCI 5TC declined by $580 to close at $41,571, reflecting the more cautious tone seen across most routes. In the Pacific, activity remained comparatively buoyant, with three miners active in the market. Conditions were described as relatively stable, with some owners reportedly able to command a slight premium for earlier laycans. Fixtures on the C5 route were concluded in the range of $11.95–$12.50, supporting the C5 index, which edged marginally higher by 0.095 to finish at $12.115. By contrast, the Atlantic experienced a quieter, more tentative start to the week, with market participants largely in an assessment mode. Sentiment in this basin appeared marginally softer. The C3 index reflected this softer tone, slipping by 0.236 to close at $24.823.
Atlantic
Sinoafrica fixed last Friday a Solebay TBN for 170,000-200,000/10 from Freetown to Qingdao for 9/13 January at $24.25. Ore and Metal also fixed on Friday a Richland TBN for 170,000/10 from Saldanha Bay to Qingdao for 1/5 January at $18.21.
Asia
Rio Tinto fixed a TBN for 170,000/10 from Dampier to Qingdao for 22/24 December at $11.95. BHP fixed a TBN for 160,000/10 from Port Hedland to Qingdao for 24/26 December at $12.05. FMG fixed a TBN for 160,000/10 from Port Hedland to Qingdao for 21/23 December at $12.50, further details have not come to light.
PANAMAX
The week opened on a softer footing with sentiment still sliding with only a handful of Atlantic fixtures reported. Trans-Atlantic and fronthaul rates are both seemingly coming under pressure with charterers continuing to test lower levels. In Asia there are currently a fair amount of prompt vessels, with charterers putting out lower bids in the hopes that a few owners may start to lock in some cover. Period activity was limited and the P5TC average closed $217 down at $16,313.
Atlantic
The Depsina V (80,737 2018) Open in Jorf Lasfar 9 December was reported to be on subjects, but further specifics lacked. The Sudety (82,138 2013) Barcelona 7/8 December was reported to have fixed for an Atlantic round voyage basis delivery passing Gibraltar and redelivery passing Gibraltar at $16,500 with messrs Bunge.
Asia
The Jade (76,596 2006) Kemen 12/14 December is rumoured to have been placed on subjects for a trip via TBCT into Putian 16/19 December with messrs Seakudos but a rate did not emerge. Likewise, the scrubber fitted Cl Qingshui He (81,064 2020) was reported to be fixed but full details have yet to be confirmed. An Oldendorff Post Panamax was reported to be on subjects for a NoPac run into China but no further details have been disclosed.
SUPRAMAX
Continuing from the end of last week it was a rather subdued start. The Atlantic remained relatively unchanged although some felt that the US Gulf was possibly slightly firmer. Otherwise, the South Atlantic remained finely balanced. From Asia, little sign of any change in direction as the negative momentum continued, brokers saying a good amount of prompt tonnage was available. The 11TC average closed down the day $79 at $18,075.
Atlantic
The Pacific Constant (61,450 2016) open Le Havre 4/5 December was heard fixed for a scrap run to the East Mediterranean, but no more details came to light. The ND Maritsa (61,272 2016) open Veracruz 12 December was heard to have been on subjects for a US Gulf trip Japan with petcoke at $30,000 to Mercuria. It also surfaced that the Sakura L (63,742 2018) open Providence 18/20 December was on subjects for US East Coast trip West Mediterranean with coal at $29,500. Elsewhere, the Silhouette Island (61,412 2011) open Onne was heard to have been failed on a Kpeme trip India at $30,000.
Asia
It surfaced that recently the Daho Nang 19 (54,958 2010) open Machong 6 December fixed a trip via Indonesia redelivery China at $15,350. Also, the Stoja (51,886 2012) open Kemaman 4/7 December fixed a trip China at around $16,000. The Chang Hang Hang Hai (58,023 2012) was heard fixed in the high $17,000s delivery Singapore via Indonesia with coal to China. The AM Ocean Pearl (56,819 2011) was heard fixed in the low $15,000s delivery Philippines also for an Indonesia trip China, again no more details surfaced.
HANDYSIZE
The week began with generally steady sentiment across the Atlantic and Asian markets. Although some fresh demand emerged in the Continent–Mediterranean, the abundance of open tonnage kept levels around last done, preventing any notable improvements. The Zante Dawn (34,146 2011) fixed from Iskenderun for a trip to NC South America at $10,500, though further details remain private. In the U.S. Gulf and South Atlantic, brokers reported continued strengthening sentiment, driven by increasing cargo availability and a tightening tonnage list that helped keep levels firm. The SE Cardi (37,717 2019) was fixed from Norfolk to Tunisia with grains at $24,000, with no additional details disclosed. Asian markets were more subdued, with reduced cargo inquiries and softer activity contributing to a mild downward drift in rates. Reflecting these regional variances, the 7TC average fell by $33 and ending the day at $15,113.
All the above is given in good faith but wog. E&OE.
Thanks and best regards.
